answer:
**Kātyāyana Smṛti chapter 47 covers the measure of interest (vṛddhi or vyaji) on loans for various commodities.**[^1]

It specifies that the principal lent by creditors is always receivable as double (dviguṇa), with recalculation if not received.[^2] Limits are set at double the principal for gems, pearls, corals, gold, silver, cotton, silk, and wool.[^3] Interest is eight-fold (*aṣṭaguṇa*) for oils, liquors, ghee, molasses, and salt.[^4] For base metals it is five-fold (*pañcaguṇa*), and for land also eight-fold; certain payments are due "immediately."[^5]

The chapter is concise (5 paragraphs, 101 words) and follows discussions on deliberation of interest (ch. 45) and related matters (ch. 46).[^6]

[^1]: Kātyāyana, *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, Chapter 47, [/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/).

[^2]: [/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/#p-16d029](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/#p-16d029).

[^3]: [/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/#p-2acf4a](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/#p-2acf4a).

[^4]: [/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/#p-bfebbd](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/#p-bfebbd).

[^5]: [/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/#p-d4145c](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/47/translation/#p-d4145c).

[^6]: Kātyāyana, *Kātyāyana Smṛti*, Chapter 47 wiki card, [wiki](/works/unknown-katyayana-smrti-1e06d2/wiki/47.card.md).
